accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text; the description of current law and the amendment is accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; note that 'submit the' and 'processing of submitted' appear to have typos (missing 'FAFSA') in the CRS text but these are formatting artifacts, not hallucinations.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose: requiring ED to make FAFSA available by October 1 each year.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly describes the amendment: removing January 1 flexibility and imposing statutory October 1 deadline.

exceptions_conditions.why: Correctly covers the certification requirement and the testimony requirement if deadline will not be met.

effective_dates.why: No enactment date stated, but this is acceptable for a high-level summary.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers both the deadline change and the certification/testimony requirements.

coherent.why: Clear and well-organized despite apparent text artifacts.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Department of Education (ED), Secretary of Education, FAFSA, and Congress.

correct_figures.why: October 1, September 1 dates are correctly stated.
